intro:
I couldn't find any reliable sources that mention the specific plant "Angraecoides keniae." However, I can provide information on Angraecum, a genus of orchids that includes species that resemble Angraecoides keniae. 

Angraecum is a genus of orchids, characterized by their long, slender stems and funnel-shaped flowers. Angraecum species, such as Angraecum eburneum, have been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may be attributed to their bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Angraecum include the potential to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. Traditional herbal preparations made from Angraecum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in African traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
